headlines.summary: Hengli Petrochemical, the privately-owned Chinese refiner that was sanctioned by the U.S. in April over allegedly buying Iranian oil, is looking to buy crude from other Middle Eastern producers and West Africa, Reuters reported on Thursday, citing trade sources. headlines.title: India Secures Crude Supply Through August with Higher UAE Imports
headlines.summary: Indian refiners have secured crude supply at least through August as they boost purchases from the United Arab Emirates (UAE), Africa, and Brazil, trade sources told Reuters on Thursday. headlines.title: Oil Could Hit $150 If U.S.-Iran Ceasefire Collapses
headlines.summary: Oil prices could hit $150 per barrel if hostilities between the United States and Iran escalate further from the current, most testing period in the ceasefire, according to intelligence firm Rystad Energy. headlines.title: Japan Taps Reserves and New Suppliers to Beat the Oil Blockade
headlines.summary: Despite the Hormuz crisis, Japan has secured the same volumes of oil imports for July as it did a year ago, as it is boosting imports from producers not dependent on the Strait to deliver their crude, sources familiar with the procurement plans told Kyodo News on Thursday. headlines.title: Trans Mountain Oil Pipeline Hits Full Capacity as Asian Demand Surges
headlines.summary: The Trans Mountain pipeline has reached full capacity for the first time since its expansion was completed, boosting the pipe’s total carrying capability to 890,000 barrels daily. headlines.title: OPEC Oil Production Falls to Lowest Level Since 2000
headlines.summary: Crude oil production among members of OPEC has this year collapsed to a generational low, at just 16.13 million barrels daily, Reuters reported, citing a regular survey it conducts on OPEC production. The amount is the lowest since 2000, the publication noted. The May average is

headlines.title: Oil Prices Spike as Iran Declares Strait of Hormuz Closed
